themes.topic: SK Hynix CEO on long-term memory shortage
themes.summary: SK Hynix CEO stated that memory demand will exceed production capacity for the next decade, with customers signing long-term contracts with minimum volume commitments and price floors, indicating a structural change in the memory market.

mentions.text:
SK HYNIX CEO: NEXT YEAR IS EXPECTED TO BE THE WORST YEAR IN THE INDUSTRY’S HISTORY FROM A SUPPLY PERSPECTIVE — RTRS

SK HYNIX CEO: DESPITE AGGRESSIVE CAPACITY EXPANSION, MEMORY DEMAND WILL CONTINUE TO EXCEED THE COMPANY’S PRODUCTION CAPACITY OVER THE NEXT DECADE — RTRS

Memory shortage to last until 2030's:

According to SK Hynix's CEO today at the Nasdaq IPO press conference.

“The rate at which demand is increasing far exceeds the speed at which we can increase supply.”

Customers are signing long-term contracts because “they believe that the
SK HYNIX CEO: MEMORY CHIP SHORTAGE MAY PERSIST PAST 2030

themes.summary: TSMC's CoWoS packaging capacity is projected to reach over 200,000 wafers per month by 2027, with 24-hour shifts to accelerate build-out, driven by demand from NVIDIA and CSPs like Google and Amazon. SoIC capacity is also ramping significantly, with AMD turning to other OSATs for additional orders.

themes.topic: HBM bit exchange rate and DRAM supply tightening
themes.summary: The bit exchange rate for HBM4E from commodity DRAM is estimated at 4-5x, meaning every incremental 1TB of HBM4E demand crowds out >4TB of commodity DRAM-equivalent wafer bit output, tightening the entire DRAM complex.

mentions.text:

themes.topic: Optical sector demand outlook and market sentiment
themes.summary: Despite short-term weakness from CPO delay reports and China capacity scares, the optical sector is expected to see rising total demand due to growing XPU numbers, with short sellers reportedly planning to close positions and buy in 2027 for CPO opportunities. VCSELs are seen as a bridge for NPO/short reach, while UHP CW is ideal for CPO.

themes.topic: CPO volume ramp dependencies and physical constraints
themes.summary: The real volume ramp for CPO is constrained by energy, heat, and reach, requiring 800V power, liquid cooling, external-laser reliability, and optical-engine qualification to be ready simultaneously.

mentions.text:
Looking at CPO through a single optical supply-chain diagram does not explain why it is arriving now, when it reaches volume, or where the margins will move.

This article redraws CPO as a system constrained by three competing physical budgets: energy, heat, and reach. The real v

themes.topic: SK Hynix ADR strong performance post-listing
themes.summary: SK Hynix's ADR ($SKHYV) saw a significant premium and strong performance post-listing, trading up 16%, potentially due to investors moving funds to the US instrument and different demand curves compared to its Korean listing.

mentions.text:

themes.topic: Taiwan's surging semiconductor exports driven by AI
themes.summary: Taiwan's semiconductor exports surged in June (+33.4% YoY) and H1 2026 (+43.9%), with DRAM exports up 158.5%, driven by blockbuster demand for AI-related goods. Chip equipment and chip imports also rose significantly.

mentions.text:

themes.topic: NVIDIA Kyber roadmap cancellation and VR PCB bottlenecks
themes.summary: NVIDIA removed Kyber from its roadmap due to manufacturing difficulties with the orthogonal backplane, while Victory Giant's HDI PCB issues (impedance mismatch) are bottlenecking Virtual Rack (VR) production, potentially causing further delays.

themes.topic: Meta's 'Iris' AI chip roadmap and supply chain
themes.summary: Meta's 4th generation in-house AI chip, 'Iris,' is set for production in September, aiming to double compute to 14GW next year. Broadcom is a design partner, TSMC will manufacture, and Meta has secured long-term supply agreements with Samsung (memory), SanDisk (flash), and Sumitomo (fiber optic).

mentions.text:
Meta will have an AI chip codenamed ‘Iris’ in production from September as part of a plan to double compute to 14GW next year, after it deploys 7GW this year, Reuters reports:
-Iris is part of a 4-generation MTIA project from its inhouse design team.

themes.topic: Apple using Intel fabs for Mac/iPhone chips due to tariff exemption
themes.summary: Apple reportedly secured an exemption from semiconductor tariffs by committing to manufacture Mac and iPhone chips at Intel's fabs.

themes.topic: Surging demand and supply constraints for Low-DK Gen 2 PCBs
themes.summary: Demand for Low-DK Gen 2 PCBs for new GPU and ASIC chips is expected to surge in 2H26, but supply chain expansion faces machinery lead times and yield issues, leading to resilient pricing and potential increases.

themes.topic: AI's impact on software development and entrepreneurship
themes.summary: AI is making software development ideas more feasible by reducing required hours and token spend, fueling the current AI build-out with near-term ROI, and setting the stage for future advancements like agents and robots.

mentions.text:

themes.topic: OpenAI disrupting major tech companies with its ecosystem
themes.summary: Sam Altman and OpenAI are seen as disruptors to major tech companies like Apple, Google, and Microsoft by building their own ecosystem rather than being absorbed into existing ones.

mentions.text:
